title: hamamelis japonica subsp. obtusata
intro: Hamamelis japonica subsp. obtusata, also known as Japanese witch hazel, is a species of flowering plant in the witch hazel family, characterized by its fragrant, spidery flowers and aromatic bark. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been shown to soothe and protect the skin. The health benefits of Japanese witch hazel include reducing inflammation, improving skin health, and relieving digestive issues, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. The plant can be prepared in various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, allowing for its benefits to be harnessed in different ways.
